hero_sub: BreatheBetter turns a simple breath into clear, understandable lung-health insight — powered by a custom ultrasonic spirometer you can build yourself.
features_sub: Three pieces working together to make respiratory health accessible to every student.
feat1_body: A custom HC-SR04 device connects straight to your browser over USB — no apps, no installs.
feat2_body: One deep breath gives you FEV1, FVC, and your FEV1/FVC ratio in seconds.
feat3_body: Every test is saved with a flow-volume curve so you can watch your trend.
how_step1_body: Plug in your Arduino spirometer and connect it with one click.
how_step2_body: Take a deep breath and blow hard into the tube. Water rises; the sensor reads it.
how_step3_body: See your status instantly and review the science behind it.
sdg_label: Supporting the UN Sustainable Development Goals
login_sub: Log in or create your account to start testing.
login_hint: First time? Enter your email and password to create your account instantly.
login_need_both: Please enter your email and password.
err_weak_pass: Password must be at least 6 characters.
err_too_many: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment.
err_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
dashboard_sub: Connect your device and run a breathing test.
serial_unsupported: Web Serial only works in Chrome or Edge. Please switch browsers to connect.
disconnected_banner: Arduino disconnected. Please replug and reconnect.
history_cta_sub: View all your past tests and trends.
step1: Upload the Arduino sketch to your device, then plug it into your laptop via USB.
step2: Click Connect Device and select your Arduino from the browser popup.
step3: The green banner confirms connection with live cm readings.
step4: Click Test, breathe in deeply, then blow out as hard and as long as you can.
step5: Your FEV1, FVC, and status appear and are saved to your account.
status_idle: Run a test to see your respiratory status.
advice_normal: No immediate concern. Your FEV1/FVC ratio is within normal range.
advice_borderline: Your ratio is slightly below 70%. Monitor closely and consult a doctor if symptoms persist.
advice_low: Your FEV1/FVC ratio is below 60%. Please consult a doctor and review your results together.
history_page_sub: Every test you've taken, with trends over time.
history_empty: No results yet. Run your first test to start tracking.
step_baseline_msg: Hold still. Do NOT breathe out yet.
step_exhale_msg: Breathe in deeply, then blow out as hard as you can!
cal_intro: Push a known volume through the tube to calibrate the sensor.
cal_blow: Blow the known volume through the tube now…
about_body_1: BreatheBetter is a lung function monitoring platform built for a student health-awareness initiative. It connects to a custom HC-SR04 ultrasonic spirometer via USB.
about_body_2: It measures how far water rises in a tube when you breathe out, converts that height into FVC and FEV1 values, then flags your respiratory status automatically.
about_body_3: Every result is saved securely to your account with a timestamp, so you can track changes over time.
about_science_1: Spirometry measures how much and how quickly you can move air out of your lungs. Three numbers tell the story:
about_def_fev1: Forced Expiratory Volume in 1 second — how much air you push out in the first second.
about_def_fvc: Forced Vital Capacity — the total air you can forcibly exhale in one breath.
about_def_ratio: The ratio of the two. A value below 70% can indicate airway obstruction.
about_hardware_1: The device pairs an Arduino with an HC-SR04 ultrasonic sensor mounted above a sealed water tube. Wire it as follows:
about_hardware_2: Upload the sketch (see the project README), open at 9600 baud, and confirm cm values are printing before connecting.
sdg3_body: Making lung-health screening accessible promotes early awareness and well-being.
sdg4_body: An open, build-it-yourself design teaches the science of respiration hands-on.
sdg10_body: Low-cost hardware brings health technology to students who'd otherwise lack access.
about_credit: Built as part of my MYP Personal Project at DPS International — designed, coded, and engineered end to end.
safe_use_sub: The ethics, rights and policies behind BreatheBetter — written to be read, not buried.
doc_charter_desc: Our commitments across three areas: the responsible use of automated systems, respect for personal autonomy, and the health rights of every individual.
doc_policies_desc: Privacy, terms, disclaimers and consent — the full policy pack governing your use of BreatheBetter.
charter_topic_1: Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence in Modern Day Systems
disclaimer: This is not medical advice. For awareness purposes only.
footer_sig: Spira. Scio. Vive. — BreatheBetter by Ryan Tyagi
